steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Small bins hold 1 fish each, so $s$ small bins hold $s \cdot 1 = s$ fish. Large bins hold 2 fish each, so $b$ large bins hold $b \cdot 2 = 2b$ fish.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The total number of fish is the sum from both bin types: $s + 2b = 43$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • $s + 2b = 43$ $s + 2b = 43$. Each small bin contributes 1 fish and each large bin contributes 2 fish, matching the setup exactly. That is the correct answer.
  • $s + b = 43$ $s + b = 43$. This would mean every bin, regardless of size, holds exactly 1 fish. Incorrect.
  • $2s + b = 43$ $2s + b = 43$. This reverses the capacities, assigning 2 fish to small bins and 1 fish to large bins. Incorrect.
  • $2s + 2b = 43$ $2s + 2b = 43$. This assumes every bin holds 2 fish, ignoring the small-bin capacity of 1. Incorrect.
